title.zh: Novartis以最高15亿美元收购Myricx，ADC继续成为大型药企补管线的高溢价赛道
title.en: Novartis buys Myricx for up to $1.5bn as ADCs remain a high-premium pipeline target for big pharma

summary.zh: FT报道，Novartis将以11亿美元首付款、最高15亿美元总价收购英国Myricx Bio，押注其新型抗体偶联药物平台。这个交易说明，ADC仍是大型药企争夺肿瘤管线的核心方向，即使资产还在早期，平台技术和靶点组合也能获得高溢价。对医药行业而言，资本正在从单一重磅药转向可扩展治疗平台。
summary.en: The Financial Times reports that Novartis will acquire UK-based Myricx Bio for $1.1bn upfront and up to $1.5bn in total, betting on its antibody-drug conjugate platform. The deal shows that ADCs remain a core oncology pipeline target for large pharma, and that even early-stage assets can command high premiums when platform technology and target optionality are credible. Capital is shifting from single blockbuster bets toward expandable therapeutic platforms.

watch.zh: 观察ADC交易是否继续从后期临床资产前移到平台收购，以及英国、美国和欧洲生物技术公司的退出路径是否继续被跨国药企主导。
watch.en: Watch whether ADC dealmaking keeps moving from late-stage clinical assets toward platform acquisitions, and whether biotech exits in the UK, US, and Europe remain dominated by multinational pharma.

liveUpdates.reference: Financial Times, Novartis to buy UK biotech Myricx Bio in $1.5bn deal, published 2026-07-07

referenceLinks.label: FT: Novartis to buy Myricx Bio

title.zh: Micron把美国投资计划提高到2500亿美元，AI内存供应链开始进入国家制造能力叙事
title.en: Micron lifts US investment plans above $250bn as AI memory supply becomes a national manufacturing-capacity story

summary.zh: Kiplinger报道，Micron计划到2035年前把美国投资提高到2500亿美元以上，并希望超过40%的DRAM在美国本土生产。AI行情的含义正在变化：HBM和DRAM不只是芯片周期品，而是云厂商、主权AI、先进封装和本土制造政策共同竞争的基础设施资源。
summary.en: Kiplinger reports that Micron plans to raise US investment above $250bn by 2035 and aims to produce more than 40% of its DRAM domestically. The meaning of the AI trade is changing: HBM and DRAM are no longer just cyclical chip products, but infrastructure resources contested by cloud providers, sovereign AI strategies, advanced packaging, and domestic manufacturing policy.

watch.zh: 后续看美国本土DRAM产能是否缓解AI内存短缺，还是进一步推高设备、人才和电力需求。
watch.en: Watch whether domestic DRAM capacity eases AI memory shortages or instead intensifies demand for equipment, talent, and power.

liveUpdates.reference: Kiplinger, Micron gives stocks a $250 billion AI boost, published 2026-07-11

title.zh: 2026智能制造AI路线图把工业AI重点从单点模型转向可靠、可解释、可集成的生产系统
title.en: The 2026 smart-manufacturing AI roadmap shifts industrial AI from isolated models to reliable, explainable, integrated production systems

summary.zh: 2026年AI/ML智能制造路线图强调，制造业AI的关键不只是预测模型，而是工业大数据、传感控制、数字孪生、供应链优化、可解释性和RAMS可靠性体系的集成。对产业判断而言，AI从办公室软件走向工厂时，价值会由能否接入设备、质量系统和维护流程决定。
summary.en: The 2026 AI/ML roadmap for smart manufacturing stresses that industrial AI is not only about predictive models, but integration across industrial data, sensing and control, digital twins, supply-chain optimisation, explainability, and RAMS reliability systems. As AI moves from office software into factories, value will depend on whether it connects to equipment, quality systems, and maintenance workflows.

watch.zh: 观察制造企业是否把AI指标纳入停机率、良率、能源效率和设备维护，而不是只做概念验证。
watch.en: Watch whether manufacturers tie AI metrics to downtime, yield, energy efficiency, and asset maintenance rather than stopping at proofs of concept.

liveUpdates.reference: Lee et al., 2026 Roadmap on AI and Machine Learning for Smart Manufacturing, arXiv:2605.00839

referenceLinks.label: arXiv: 2026 Roadmap on AI and ML for Smart Manufacturing

title.zh: 大型视觉模型在土木裂缝识别上仍有盲区，基础设施AI不能直接照搬互联网图像模型
title.en: Foundation vision models still miss civil cracks, so infrastructure AI cannot simply reuse internet-image models

evidence.zh: 研究类型：2026年数据集论文；内容：CiF约15万张高分辨率基础设施图像，用于实例级缺陷分割与foundation model评估。
evidence.en: Evidence: 2026 dataset paper introducing CiF, about 150,000 high-resolution civil-infrastructure images for instance-level defect segmentation and foundation-model evaluation.

summary.zh: 这项研究指出，当前foundation models和vision-language models在基础设施裂缝、剥落、钢筋外露等细粒度缺陷上仍表现有限，原因包括材料表面纹理弱、缺陷形状细长、标注成本高和互联网图像预训练偏差。对结构工程师的启发是，AI巡检不能只看模型名气；必须关注数据来源、标注质量、缺陷定义和误检后果。未来真正可用的结构视觉AI，需要与工程检查等级、维修阈值和责任链一起设计。
summary.en: This study shows that current foundation and vision-language models still struggle with cracks, spalling, exposed reinforcement, and other fine-grained civil defects. Causes include weak material texture, elongated defect geometry, costly annotation, and pretraining bias from internet images. For structural engineers, the lesson is that AI inspection should not be judged by model reputation alone; data source, annotation quality, defect definition, and false-decision consequences matter. Useful structural vision AI must be designed together with inspection grades, repair thresholds, and responsibility chains.

caveat.zh: 数据集极有价值，但跨国家材料、施工方法和拍摄设备的泛化仍需更多验证。
caveat.en: The dataset is valuable, but generalisation across countries, materials, construction practices, and imaging devices still needs further validation.

insights.reference: Farronato et al., Cracks in the Foundation, arXiv:2605.18413

title.zh: 桥梁AI巡检不仅要识别病害，还要处理地点匿名化和公众风险沟通
title.en: AI bridge inspection must detect damage while handling location anonymisation and public-risk communication

evidence.zh: 研究类型：2026年系统实现论文；方法：SAM3、DBSCAN、OCR预处理、施工标志模糊和桥梁病害图像处理。
evidence.en: Evidence: 2026 system implementation using SAM3, DBSCAN, OCR preprocessing, construction-sign blurring, and bridge-damage image processing.

summary.zh: 日本桥梁巡检研究把病害识别与地点匿名化放在同一系统里，原因很现实：现场照片可能包含施工牌、区域信息和公众可识别线索，直接公开会引发安全与舆情问题。这个方向提醒工程团队，结构健康监测不是单纯的检测精度竞赛。实际部署还要考虑数据脱敏、图像治理、维修优先级可视化和公众沟通。数字化巡检越接近真实基础设施，越需要把技术、隐私和管理流程合并设计。
summary.en: This Japanese bridge-inspection study places damage detection and location anonymisation in one system for a practical reason: field photos may contain construction signs, regional information, and publicly recognisable cues that can create safety or communication issues if released directly. The point for engineering teams is that SHM is not just a detection-accuracy contest. Deployment also needs data sanitisation, image governance, repair-priority visualisation, and public communication. The closer digital inspection gets to real infrastructure, the more technical, privacy, and management workflows must be designed together.

caveat.zh: 该系统聚焦图像处理与匿名化，工程承载力判断仍需结合现场复核和结构模型。
caveat.en: The system focuses on image processing and anonymisation; engineering capacity decisions still need field review and structural modelling.

insights.reference: Yasuno, Multi-stage Bridge Inspection System, arXiv:2601.17254

title.zh: 多智能体结构工程并不是替代签字工程师，而是把计算、校核和追溯流程重新编排
title.en: Multi-agent structural engineering is not a replacement for the signing engineer, but a reorganisation of calculation, checking, and traceability

evidence.zh: 研究类型：结构工程多智能体框架；方法：LLM agents、工具调用、规范解释、荷载计算和构件承载力校核。
evidence.en: Evidence: structural-engineering multi-agent framework using LLM agents, tool calls, code interpretation, load calculation, and member-capacity checking.

summary.zh: MASSE展示了LLM agent如何把规范读取、荷载计算、构件验算和结果整理串成半自动工作流。真正的行业启发不是“AI替代结构工程师”，而是重复计算、规范检索和初步校核会被重组，工程师的价值更集中在输入边界、模型假定、异常判断和最终责任。对未来职业能力而言，懂结构原理还不够，还要能设计可审计的AI工作流，保证每一步计算可追溯、可复核、可签字。
summary.en: MASSE shows how LLM agents can connect code reading, load calculation, member checking, and result reporting into a semi-automated workflow. The industry signal is not that AI replaces structural engineers; repetitive calculation, code retrieval, and preliminary checking will be reorganised, while engineering value concentrates on input boundaries, modelling assumptions, exception judgement, and final responsibility. Future competence requires not only structural theory, but auditable AI workflows where every calculation step is traceable, reviewable, and signable.

caveat.zh: 论文是概念验证，实际工程采用还需要权限控制、版本记录、独立复核和专业责任制度。
caveat.en: The paper is a proof of concept; real practice still needs access control, version records, independent checking, and professional-liability rules.

insights.reference: Liang et al., Automating Structural Engineering Workflows with Large Language Model Agents, arXiv:2510.11004

referenceLinks.label: arXiv: Automating Structural Engineering Workflows with LLM Agents

title.zh: 楼板振动舒适度：为什么中外审查不能只看强度和挠度，还要问人会不会感觉到振动？
title.en: Floor vibration comfort: why cross-code review cannot stop at strength and deflection, but must ask whether occupants feel motion

text.zh: 楼板设计常被简化成强度和静挠度问题，但办公室、实验室、医院和住宅的真实使用体验还受振动控制。中国、澳洲、欧洲和美国文件对楼板振动的表达不完全相同：有的以自振频率、峰值加速度、步行激励和阻尼为入口，有的采用经验限值或专门设计指南。核心物理一致：人对低幅高频或低频重复振动很敏感，即使构件强度安全、挠度满足限值，也可能因为脚步、设备或节奏性活动产生舒适度投诉。跨规范复核时，应把质量、刚度、阻尼、跨度、楼面用途和激励类型一起说明。
text.en: Floor design is often simplified into strength and static deflection, but offices, laboratories, hospitals, and residential floors are also governed by vibration comfort. Chinese, Australian, European, and US documents express floor vibration differently: some start from natural frequency, peak acceleration, walking excitation, and damping, while others use empirical limits or specialist design guides. The physics is the same: occupants are sensitive to repeated low-amplitude motion, and a floor that is safe in strength and static deflection may still attract complaints under walking, equipment, or rhythmic activity. Cross-code review should state mass, stiffness, damping, span, floor use, and excitation type together.

text.zh: 强度验算回答会不会破坏；振动验算回答人在正常使用中是否能接受。两者属于不同极限状态，不能用一个静挠度限值完全替代。
text.en: Strength checks answer whether failure is avoided; vibration checks answer whether normal use is acceptable to occupants. They are different limit states, and a static deflection limit cannot fully replace vibration assessment.

text.zh: 振动问题对质量、刚度和阻尼非常敏感。加厚楼板会增加刚度，也会增加质量；隔墙、吊顶和家具可能增加阻尼，但施工阶段空楼板通常更不利。
text.en: Vibration is sensitive to mass, stiffness, and damping. Thickening a slab increases stiffness but also mass; partitions, ceilings, and furniture may add damping, while bare floors during construction are often less favourable.

text.zh: 国际报告常写 natural frequency、modal mass、damping ratio、response factor 或 peak acceleration；中文说明更常把它放在舒适度、使用性能或专项振动验算里。术语不同，但目标都是控制人体感知。
text.en: International reports often use natural frequency, modal mass, damping ratio, response factor, or peak acceleration. Chinese notes more often frame the issue under comfort, serviceability, or a specialist vibration check. The wording differs, but the target is human perception.

takeaway.zh: 做楼板跨体系复核时，先判断该楼面是否可能被振动控制：长跨、轻质、高强材料、低阻尼、办公或实验室用途都需要提高警惕。然后再选择相应指南或规范方法，而不是只看强度和L/限值。
takeaway.en: In cross-system floor review, first decide whether vibration may govern: long span, lightweight systems, high-strength materials, low damping, office use, and laboratory use all raise concern. Then choose the appropriate guide or code method rather than relying only on strength and L/span limits.

title.zh: 实例：同一楼板，频率从刚度和质量的平方根关系开始判断
title.en: Example: start floor vibration screening from the square-root relation between stiffness and mass

intro.zh: 设某简化单自由度楼板等效刚度 k = 12 MN/m，等效质量 m = 18,000 kg，阻尼比先不参与频率估算。这里是概念筛查，不代替AISC、SCI或项目规范的完整振动验算。
intro.en: Assume a simplified single-degree floor with equivalent stiffness k = 12 MN/m and equivalent mass m = 18,000 kg. Damping is not included in this first frequency estimate. This is conceptual screening, not a replacement for a full AISC, SCI, or project-specific vibration assessment.

citation.zh: 推导关系：单自由度自由振动基本式；依据结构动力学教材与AISC Design Guide 11 的楼板振动筛查思想（非规范编号公式）。
citation.en: Derived relation: fundamental single-degree free-vibration expression; based on structural dynamics texts and the screening logic in AISC Design Guide 11 (not a numbered code equation).

citation.zh: 推导关系：圆频率与Hz频率换算；结构动力学基本关系（非规范编号公式）。
citation.en: Derived relation: conversion between circular frequency and frequency in Hz; basic structural dynamics relation (not a numbered code equation).

result.zh: fn = 25.8 / (2π) = 4.1 Hz。这个量级接近步行激励敏感区，不能只靠静挠度判断。
result.en: fn = 25.8 / (2π) = 4.1 Hz. This is close to the walking-excitation sensitive range, so static deflection alone is insufficient.

citation.zh: 推导关系：质量不变时由频率公式直接得到；用于方案阶段理解刚度调整影响（非规范编号公式）。
citation.en: Derived relation: obtained directly from the frequency expression when mass is unchanged; useful for concept-stage understanding of stiffness changes (not a numbered code equation).

result.zh: 若质量不变、刚度提高44%，频率只提高√1.44 = 1.2倍。说明振动优化通常需要同时考虑刚度、质量、阻尼和激励源。
result.en: If mass is unchanged and stiffness increases by 44%, frequency rises only by √1.44 = 1.2 times. Vibration improvement usually requires combined treatment of stiffness, mass, damping, and excitation source.

note.zh: 这个例子说明，楼板振动首先是动力问题。强度安全和静挠度合格只是底线，办公、住宅、实验室和健身空间还需要按用途检查频率、加速度、阻尼和人群激励。
note.en: This example shows that floor vibration is first a dynamics problem. Strength safety and acceptable static deflection are only baseline requirements; offices, apartments, laboratories, and fitness spaces also need checks on frequency, acceleration, damping, and human excitation.

classroom.references: AISC Design Guide 11; SCI P354; structural dynamics floor vibration screening literature

referenceLinks.label: AISC Design Guide 11: Vibrations of Steel-Framed Structural Systems Due to Human Activity
SCI P354: Design of floors for vibration
